subject: You're probably paying 2x more per hire than you need to
body:
Hi [Name],

Average cost-per-hire in tech is $4,700 according to SHRM. But companies that have built structured internal talent pipelines cut that number to $1,800–2,200 — same quality, half the spend.

The difference isn't budget. It's whether you're systematically building a bench or starting from zero each time a role opens.

[Product] helps you build and maintain that bench automatically — tracking passive candidates, alumni, and referrals in one place.

One CEO told me they filled their last 3 senior roles from the bench without a single recruiter call.

15 minutes this week?
why_it_works: data-driven + story close — SHRM benchmark + specific savings + social proof anecdote
